passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. Nos. 16-19) Read the following information and answer the questions that follow. In a study of five brands of pain relieving tablets P, Q, R, S and T, the brands were tested and ranked against each other as more or less effective per dose. The following results were obtained. I. P was more effective than Q. II. The effectiveness of R was less than that of S. III. T was the least effective brand tested. IV. Q and R were equally effective. V. The effectiveness of S was greater than that of Q.

Directions (Q. Nos. 20-25) Read the following information carefully and answer the questions that follow. Six compounds are being tested for possible use in a new anti-poison, 'Sweet 'N' Deadly'. I. U is sweeter than V and more deadly than Z. II. V is sweeter than Y and less deadly than Z. III. W is less sweet than X and less deadly than U. IV. X is less sweet and more deadly than Y. V. Y is less sweet and more deadly than U. VI. Z is sweeter than U and less deadly than W.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the position of Radha from the shorter end?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following statements is definitely correct?

choices.a: Bina occupies the third position from the top

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the above statements are true, which of the following must also be true?

choices.a: P and S were equally effective
choices.d: R was less effective than P

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: All the informations in the results given above can be derived from which of the following groups of statements?

choices.d: Statements I, II, III and IV

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If a sixth brand M is tested and found to be more effective than S, then which of the following must be true, if the findings of the study are correct?

choices.a: M is the most effective of all the six brands tested
choices.b: Atleast four of the six brands tested are less effective than M
choices.c: M is more effective than P
choices.d: M is less effective than P

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If R is more expensive per dose than P and T is less expensive per dose than R, which of the following must be true, according to the study, for a consumer, who wishes to buy a pain reliever with the greatest effectiveness for the amount spent per dose?

choices.a: P should be purchased instead of R
choices.b: P should be purchased instead of T
choices.c: T should be purchased instead of R
choices.d: Q should be purchased instead of R, if Q is of the same price as S

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following is/are both sweeter and more deadly than V?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following statement adds no new information about sweetness to the statements that precede it?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following is/are sweeter than Y and more deadly than W?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Mukesh is taller than Suresh but shorter than Rakesh. Rakesh is taller than Harish but shorter than Amar. Who among them is the shortest with regards to height?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Roshan is taller than Hardik who is shorter than Sushil. Niza is taller than Harry but shorter than Hardik. Sushil is shorter than Roshan. Who is the tallest?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Among five boys, Vasant is taller than Manohar but not as tall as Raju. Jayant is taller than Dutta but shorter than Manohar. Who is the tallest in the group?
